ci_support_linux_64_c_stdlib_version2.17:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
hdf5:
- 1.14.6
libboost_headers:
- '1.88'
libitk_devel:
- '5.4'
numpy:
- '2'
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
root_base:
- 6.36.8
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
  - c_stdlib_version

about.description:
STIR is Open Source software for use in tomographic imaging.
Its aim is to provide a Multi-Platform Object-Oriented framework for all data manipulations in tomographic imaging.
Check the recipe at https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/recipe.yaml
to see which dependencies are included in this conda package.

This package contains all STIR utilities and Python support. It is built with ITK on all platforms
and (where supported) ROOT on Linux and OSX.

name: stir

about.description:
STIR is Open Source software for use in tomographic imaging.
Its aim is to provide a Multi-Platform Object-Oriented framework for all data manipulations in tomographic imaging.
Check the recipe at https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/recipe.yaml
to see which dependencies are included in this conda package.

This package contains all STIR utilities and Python support. It is built with ITK on all platforms

STIR is Open Source software for use in tomographic imaging.
Its aim is to provide a Multi-Platform Object-Oriented framework for all data manipulations in tomographic imaging.
Check the recipe at https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/recipe.yaml
to see which dependencies are included in this conda package.

This package contains all STIR utilities and Python support. It is built with ITK on all platforms
and (where supported) ROOT on Linux and OSX.

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  name: STIR
  version: "6.3.1"
  build_number: 0

  cuda_version: ${{ env.get("CONDA_OVERRIDE_CUDA", default="None") }}
  with_cuda: ${{ "true" if cuda_version != "None" else "" }}
  cuda_major: ${{ (cuda_version | split(".") | first) if with_cuda else "0" }}
  cuda_build_prefix: ${{ "cuda" ~ (cuda_compiler_version | version_to_buildstring) if with_cuda else "cpu" }}

package:
  name: ${{ name | lower }}
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  git: https://github.com/UCL/STIR.git
  tag: rel_${{ version }}

build:
  number: ${{ build_number + 200 if with_cuda else build_number }}
  # Don't support ROOT 6.38.00 as it caused segfaults in
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/pull/153
  skip:
    - match(root_base, ">=6.38")
  string: ${{ cuda_build_prefix }}_h${{ hash }}_${{ build_number + 200 if with_cuda else build_number }}
  variant:
    use_keys:
      - ${{ "cuda" if with_cuda }}
    down_prioritize_variant: ${{ 0 if with_cuda else 1 }}
  script:
    file: build
    env:
      cuda_compiler_version: ${{ cuda_version }}
requirements:
  ignore_run_exports:
    by_name:
      - cudatoolkit
  build:
    - ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - if: with_cuda
      then: ${{ compiler('cuda') }}
    - if: osx
      then: llvm-openmp
    - if: linux
      then: libgomp
    - cmake
    - ninja
    - swig
  host:
    - libboost-headers
    - if: not win and not (osx and arm64)
      then: hdf5
    - python
    - numpy
    - libitk-devel
    - libitk
    - nlohmann_json
    - if: with_cuda
      then:
        - cuda-version ==${{ cuda_version }}
    - if: with_cuda and (cuda_major | int) >= 12 and linux
      then: cuda-driver-dev
    - if: with_cuda and (cuda_major | int) >= 12
      then: cuda-cudart-dev
    - libparallelproj <2.0 
    - if: not win
      then: root_base
    - if: win
      then: zlib
  run:
    - python
    - if: with_cuda
      then:
        - __cuda
        - cuda-version ==${{ cuda_version }}
    # Needed for developers, see https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/issues/120
    - libboost-headers
    - libitk-devel
    - libparallelproj

tests:
  - script:
      - stir_config --config-dir
      - cd recon_test_pack
      - if: not win
        then:
          # use ugly work-around to avoid the next test to run CUDA tests
          - env TRAVIS=disable_cuda_tests ./run_test_simulate_and_recon.sh
          - ./run_test_listmode_recon.sh
          - ./run_test_simulate_and_recon_with_motion.sh
          - ./run_scatter_tests.sh
          - ./run_tests.sh --nointbp
          - ./run_test_zoom_image.sh
          - ./run_root_GATE.sh
          - cd SPECT
          - ./run_SPECT_tests.sh
          - cd ..
      - if: win
        then:
          - run_tests
      - cd ..
      - pytest src/swig/test/python
    requirements:
      run:
        - pytest
        - matplotlib
    files:
      source:
        - recon_test_pack
        - examples
        - src/swig/test/python

about:
  homepage: https://stir.sourceforge.net/
  license: Apache-2.0 AND LicenseRef-PARAPET-license
  license_file: LICENSE.txt
  summary: Software for Tomographic Image Reconstruction
  description: |
    STIR is Open Source software for use in tomographic imaging.
    Its aim is to provide a Multi-Platform Object-Oriented framework for all data manipulations in tomographic imaging.
    Check the recipe at https://github.com/conda-forge/stir-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/recipe.yaml
    to see which dependencies are included in this conda package.

    This package contains all STIR utilities and Python support. It is built with ITK on all platforms
    and (where supported) ROOT on Linux and OSX.
  documentation: https://stir.sourceforge.net/documentation
  repository: https://github.com/UCL/STIR

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - KrisThielemans
    - paskino
    - casperdcl
